title: Shattering Wood-Fired Tunisian Apples Bake
description: A bitter dessert featuring Braeburn apples, perfect for a lazy Sunday evening. The wood-fired bake gives the dish a unique smoky flavor.
instructions: Peel, core, and slice the Braeburn apples into thin wedges.
In a large bowl, mix together the almond flour, coconut sugar, eggs, vanilla extract, and salt until well combined.
Add the harissa to the bowl and mix until the dough is smooth.
On a comal, place the coconut oil in the center and evenly distribute the dough around it, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Arrange the apple slices on top of the dough, leaving a small gap between each slice.
Bake the apples for 20-25 minutes, or until they are tender and the dough is golden brown.
Remove the comal from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing the apples and serving.
Cut the apples into wedges and serve warm, topped with a dollop of coconut whipped cream if desired.
